q.en: How old was Bob Gates when he made his first jump?
q.zh: 鲍勃·盖茨第一次跳伞时几岁？

options.en: 10 according to some versions of the story

explain.en: Bob says, 'I was 13' when he made his first jump.
explain.zh: 鲍勃说，他第一次跳伞时“13岁”。

q.en: What does 'the arch' mean in skydiving?
q.zh: 在跳伞中，“the arch”（弓身）是什么意思？

options.en: Spreading out and pushing your hips forward to stay stable
options.zh: 张开身体并向前顶胯以保持稳定

options.en: A type of parachute according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 一种降落伞（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The glossary defines arch as 'a skydiving position in which a person falls stomach-down with the hips thrust forward.'
explain.zh: 词汇表将arch定义为“跳伞者面朝下、向前顶胯下落的姿势”。

q.en: Why can't skydivers jump higher than 13,000 feet?
q.zh: 为什么跳伞者不能从高于13,000英尺的高度跳下？

options.en: There is not enough oxygen in the air
The plane cannot fly that high according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 飞机飞不了那么高（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Bob says, 'You can't go higher than 13,000 feet because there's not enough oxygen in the air.'
explain.zh: 鲍勃说：“你不能高于13,000英尺，因为空气中的氧气不足。”

q.en: What happens if a skydiver does NOT do the arch?
q.zh: 如果跳伞者不做出“弓身”动作会怎样？

options.en: They can flip and get tangled in the lines
options.zh: 他们会翻滚并被绳索缠住

options.en: The chute opens immediately according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 降落伞立刻打开（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Bob says, 'You can flip. If you're flipping when the chute's opening, you can get tangled in the lines.'
explain.zh: 鲍勃说：“你会翻滚。如果在开伞时正在翻滚，就会被绳索缠住。”

q.en: How long does a free fall from 13,000 feet last?
q.zh: 从13,000英尺高度自由落体大约持续多久？

options.en: About 10 minutes according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 约10分钟（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Bob says a free fall from 13,000 feet lasts 'Just a whole minute.'
explain.zh: 鲍勃说，从13,000英尺自由落体“大约整整一分钟”。

q.en: Why does Bob Gates keep skydiving?
q.zh: 鲍勃·盖茨为什么一直坚持跳伞？

options.zh: 他喜欢那种肾上腺素飙升的刺激感

options.en: He has to for his job according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 这是他的工作要求（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Bob says, 'We skydivers love the feeling of extreme excitement—the adrenaline rush—when we jump.'
explain.zh: 鲍勃说：“我们跳伞者喜欢那种极度兴奋的感觉——跳伞时的肾上腺素飙升。”

options.en: Ropes on either side used to steer the parachute
options.zh: 两侧用来操控降落伞的绳索

options.en: The reserve parachute according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 备用降落伞（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The glossary defines toggle ropes as 'ropes on either side of a parachute that allow the skydiver to steer.'
explain.zh: 词汇表将toggles定义为“降落伞两侧的绳索，供跳伞者操控方向”。

q.en: According to the trivia, when were parachutes first used to jump from airplanes?
q.zh: 根据书中的小知识，降落伞最早是在什么时候被用于从飞机上跳下的？

explain.en: The trivia says, 'Parachutes were first used to jump from airplanes in the early 1900s.'
explain.zh: 小知识说：“降落伞最早在20世纪初被用于从飞机上跳下。”

q.en: How much does skydiving training cost?
q.zh: 跳伞训练的费用是多少？

options.en: $2,000 to $4,000 according to some versions of the story

explain.en: Bob says a skydiver spends '$1,000 to $1,500' for the training course (the parachute itself costs $2,000-$4,000).
explain.zh: 鲍勃说，跳伞者要在训练课程上花费“1,000至1,500美元”（降落伞本身需2,000至4,000美元）。

q.en: What lesson does the book mainly share?
q.zh: 本书主要传达了什么道理？

options.en: With good training and trust in equipment, skydivers can handle hard situations and enjoy the thrill
options.zh: 凭借良好的训练和对装备的信任，跳伞者能应对困难局面并享受刺激

options.en: Skydiving is dangerous and should be avoided according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 跳伞很危险，应该避免（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

explain.en: The book notes, 'Good training and faith in the equipment will allow a skydiver to handle almost any difficult situation.'
explain.zh: 书中指出：“良好的训练和对装备的信任，能让跳伞者应对几乎任何困难局面。”

prompt.en: Pretend you interviewed a skydiver like Bob Gates. Write three questions you would ask and what you hope to learn.
prompt.zh: 假设你要采访一位像鲍勃·盖茨这样的跳伞者。写出你想问的三个问题，以及你希望从中了解到什么。

en: Think about safety, training, and feelings.
Explain why their answers would be interesting.
zh: 思考安全、训练和感受方面的问题。
解释为什么他们的回答会很有趣。
